 {"id":"gt-47tq","title":"gt spawn should use bd mol run for molecule attachment","description":"Simplify Gas Town to use bd mol run for work tracking.

## Key Insight
Two distinct mechanisms, not duplicative:

| Mechanism | Purpose | Query |
|-----------|---------|-------|
| **Pinned molecule** (bd mol run) | What am I working on? | `bd list --pinned --assignee=me` |
| **Handoff mail** | Context notes for restart | `gt mail read` (self-addressed) |

The handoff is just **mail to yourself** - optional context notes.
The molecule is **the actual work** - required state.

## Current State (Overengineered)
Gas Town has custom attachment system:
- Permanent "Foo Handoff" pinned beads per identity
- AttachMolecule(pinnedBeadID, moleculeID) 
- Attachment fields parsed from description
- Separate from beads pinning

## New Model (Simplified)
Use bd mol run directly:

```bash
# Spawn polecat with molecule
bd mol run mol-polecat-work --var issue=gt-xyz
# This: spawns, assigns to caller, pins root, sets in_progress
```

Query current work:
```bash
bd list --pinned --assignee=gastown/furiosa --status=in_progress
```

Handoff context (when needed):
```bash
gt mail send gastown/furiosa -s "Context notes" -m "Was on step 4..."
```

## Changes Required

### Remove from Gas Town
- AttachMolecule() / DetachMolecule()
- AttachmentFields struct and parsing
- GetAttachment() / SetAttachmentFields()
- Permanent pinned handoff beads per identity
- Daemon attachment detection (checkDeaconAttachment)

### Update gt spawn
```go
// Old: custom molecule instantiation + attachment
// New: just call bd mol run
cmd := exec.Command("bd", "mol", "run", protoID, "--var", "issue="+issueID)
```

### Update gt prime / agent context
```go
// Old: find handoff bead, parse attachment
// New: query for pinned molecule
cmd := exec.Command("bd", "list", "--pinned", "--assignee="+identity, "--status=in_progress", "--json")
```

### Update documentation
- Remove handoff bead attachment docs
- Clarify: handoff = mail, molecule = work
- Update CLAUDE.md templates

## Benefits
1. One system for work tracking (beads)
2. Simpler Gas Town code
3. bd mol squash works naturally
4. Handoff is just mail (already works)

## Related
- gt-3x0z: Wisp Molecule Integration
- gt-rana: Patrol System
- gt-lek6: gt rig reset --stale
- gt-ay1r: gt molecule current","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:34:21.808261-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T21:59:54.041276-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T21:59:54.041276-08:00","close_reason":"Simplified spawn.go to use bd mol run"}

 {"id":"gt-48bs","title":"gt rig reset: clear stale mail on reset/land","description":"## Problem

When resetting or landing a rig/town, stale mail messages can confuse agents on startup. Old handoff messages, daemon notifications, and inter-agent mail should be cleaned up as part of reset.

## Current State

- `gt rig reset` exists but doesn't clear mail
- Stale messages accumulate (e.g., daemon SHUTDOWN messages)
- Agents may read outdated context on startup

## Proposed Behavior

`gt rig reset` and `gt town reset` should:
1. Close all open messages (`--type=message`) in the relevant beads
2. Optionally preserve pinned handoff beads (clear content, keep bead)
3. Log what was cleaned up

```bash
gt rig reset gastown          # Clears gastown mail
gt rig reset gastown --mail   # Only clear mail, keep other state
gt town reset                 # Clears all town-level mail
gt town reset --all           # Clears mail in all rigs too
```

## Implementation

1. Query `bd list --type=message --status=open`
2. Close each with reason 'Cleared during reset'
3. For pinned handoffs: `bd update <id> --description=''` instead of close","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T11:42:17.769674-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T11:27:49.532203-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T11:27:49.532203-08:00","close_reason":"Added --mail flag to gt rig reset"}

 {"id":"gt-4eim","title":"gt nudge should accept flexible session identifiers","description":"Currently `gt nudge` requires the exact tmux session name (e.g., `gt-gastown-furiosa`).

It should also accept:
- `gastown/furiosa` (rig/polecat format)
- `furiosa` (polecat name, infer rig from cwd or require if ambiguous)

The session list command shows `gastown/furiosa` format, but nudge rejects it:
```
gt session list → shows 'gastown/furiosa'
gt nudge gastown/furiosa 'msg' → 'session not found'
gt nudge gt-gastown-furiosa 'msg' → works
```

Should normalize all these formats to the tmux session name internally.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"gastown/angharad","created_at":"2025-12-21T15:36:45.013475-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:37:59.042119-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-4ev4","title":"Implement gt sling command","description":"The unified work dispatch command.

```bash
gt sling <thing> <target> [options]
```

Implements spawn + assign + pin in one operation. See sling-design.md.

Acceptance:
- [ ] Parse thing (proto name, issue ID, epic ID)
- [ ] Parse target (agent address)  
- [ ] Spawn molecule if proto
- [ ] Assign to target agent
- [ ] Pin to agent's hook (pinned bead)
- [ ] Support --wisp flag for ephemeral
- [ ] Support --molecule flag for issue+workflow
- [ ] Error if hook already occupied (unless --force)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/furiosa","created_at":"2025-12-22T03:17:27.273013-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T12:33:43.076291-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T12:33:43.076291-08:00","close_reason":"Closed"}
 {"id":"gt-4my","title":"Doctor check: Worker health and stuck detection","description":"Detect and report stuck workers via gt doctor.

## Checks

### WorkerHealthCheck
- List all active workers (polecats with state=working)
- Check last activity timestamp for each
- Flag as potentially stuck if no progress for configurable threshold (default: 30 min)
- Check if Witness is running for the rig
- Verify Witness last heartbeat time

### Stuck Detection Criteria
- Polecat state=working but session not running
- Polecat state=working but output unchanged for threshold
- Witness not responding to health checks
- Multiple polecats in same rig all stuck

## Output

```
[WARN] Workers in rig 'wyvern' may be stuck:
  - Toast: working for 45m, no recent output
  - Capable: working for 52m, session not found
  - Witness: last heartbeat 20m ago
  
  Suggestions:
  - gt witness status wyvern
  - gt capture wyvern/Toast 50
  - gt stop --rig wyvern (kill all)
```

## Auto-Fix

Cannot auto-fix stuck workers (risk of data loss), but can:
- Restart Witness daemon if crashed
- Send warning mail to Mayor","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:17:59.265062-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:24:34.882466-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4my","depends_on_id":"gt-f9x.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:19:05.565606-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-4my","depends_on_id":"gt-7ik","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T15:44:42.068149-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-7oow","title":"gt mail: Cross-level routing is broken","description":"**Problem:**
When Mayor (at ~/gt) sends mail to a rig worker (gastown/crew/max), the message lands in town-level beads (~/.beads/) but the recipient checks rig-level beads (crew/max/.beads/).

**Reproduction:**
```bash
cd ~/gt
gt mail send gastown/crew/max -s 'Test' -m 'body'
# Message goes to ~/gt/.beads/ with prefix hq-*

cd ~/gt/gastown/crew/max  
gt mail inbox
# Does NOT see the message - it's looking in crew/max/.beads/
```

**Root cause:**
`findBeadsWorkDir()` walks up from CWD to find .beads. `Router.Send()` runs `bd create` in that directory. This means messages always go to the sender's beads, not the recipient's.

**Fix options:**
1. Route based on recipient address - if sending to rig/*, use that rig's .beads
2. Use a single shared beads database for all mail (simpler but less isolated)
3. Teach agents to check both levels (workaround, not fix)

**Related:**
- gt-ngu1: Pinned beads sorting (done but pointless if mail doesn't route)
- This blocks all cross-level mail functionality","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:57:30.258991-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T18:35:54.390121-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T18:35:54.390121-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-7we","title":"Swarms of One: Lightweight single-worker task dispatch","description":"Design and implement a lightweight pattern for firing off single workers to handle tasks without full swarm overhead.

## Context

Currently we have:
- town spawn: Creates a polecat in a rig with issue assignment
- Swarms (sw-*): Full lifecycle tracking with manifest, state, events, reports
- Ephemeral rigs (rig-*): Temporary worker groups for swarms

What's missing: A simple way to say 'fire off a worker to do X' without swarm ceremony.

## Design Questions

1. Should this be a new command like 'gt fire' or an option on existing commands?
2. Should single tasks still get swarm IDs (sw-N) for consistency/queryability?
3. Should it default to creating new workers or support --reuse for idle polecats?
4. How does this relate to crew workers (gt-cik)?

## Possible Interface

gt fire --rig gastown --issue gt-xyz [--prompt '...']
gt fire gastown/QuickTask --issue gt-xyz

## Related

- gt-cik: Overseer Crew (user-managed persistent workspaces)
- gt-kmn: Swarm System epic","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T16:51:02.716629-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:18.589027-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:18.589027-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-82y","title":"Design: Swarm shutdown and worker cleanup","description":"Design for graceful swarm shutdown, worker cleanup, and session cycling.

## Key Decisions

1. Pre-kill verification uses model intelligence (not framework rules)
2. Witness can request restart when context filling (mail self, exit)
3. Mayor NOT involved in per-worker cleanup (Witness responsibility)
4. Clear responsibility boundaries between Mayor/Witness/Polecat

## Subtasks (implementation)

- gt-sd6: Polecat decommission checklist prompting
- gt-f8v: Witness pre-kill verification protocol
- gt-eu9: Witness session cycling and handoff
- gt-gl2: Mayor vs Witness cleanup responsibilities

**Design complete.** Each subtask has full specification in its description.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:47:44.936374-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:49:22.849598-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-15T20:12:05.441911-08:00"}

**Gas Town implementation**: Post-work hook in molecule:
```yaml
post_work:
  discover:
    - bugs
    - punted_items
    - follow_on_work
  file_as: beads
```

Polecat output gets analyzed by AI, discovered work becomes beads issues.

**Value**: Nothing gets forgotten. VC found ~25% more issues with refinement.

 {"id":"gt-q6lg","title":"mol-crew-session: Startup/shutdown protocols for crew workers","description":"Crew workers (like joe, max) don't have patrol molecules keeping them fresh. When gt gets rebuilt, they have stale binaries that cause hangs and bugs.

## Problem

- Crew binaries get stale when gt is rebuilt elsewhere
- No automatic pull/rebase/rebuild on session start
- No standardized shutdown protocol (sync, push, handoff)

## Solution: mol-crew-session

A molecule template for crew sessions:

### Startup Phase
1. `git pull --rebase` - get latest code
2. `bd sync` - sync beads
3. `go build -o gt ./cmd/gt` - rebuild gt (if in gastown)
4. `gt prime` - load context

### Work Phase  
- Open-ended human interaction
- No molecule steps - just work until done

### Shutdown Phase
1. `git status` - check for uncommitted changes
2. `bd sync` - sync beads
3. `git push` - push code
4. Handoff if incomplete work

## Implementation

1. Define mol-crew-session in builtin_molecules.go
2. Update crew CLAUDE.md to reference the protocol
3. Optionally: gt prime auto-runs startup steps

## Dependencies

- Should implement after deacon/witness/polecat patrols are stable
- Consider: gt-3x0z.10 (Witness patrol molecules)

## Related

- gt-3x0z.9: Deacon wisp patrol (done)
- fix-gt script: Current workaround for binary freshness","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T02:48:25.658692-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T02:48:25.658692-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-q8du","title":"Configuration in Beads: use pinned beads for rig/agent config","description":"## Summary

Move configuration from JSON files into Beads data plane using pinned beads.

## Motivation

- Config becomes versionable and syncable like issues
- Agents can read config via `bd show`
- Unified data model for everything
- Supports the 'Beads as Universal Data Plane' vision (gt-aqm)

## Config Types to Migrate

### Rig-level (pinned per rig)
- Theme/colors for tmux status line
- Default branch naming conventions
- Merge queue settings
- Witness thresholds

### Agent-level (pinned per role)
- Handoffs (already planned - gt-cu7r)
- Agent preferences
- Current focus/context

### Town-level (pinned at town root)
- Cross-rig settings
- Federation config
- Global themes

## Implementation

1. Implement StatusPinned (beads-6v2) ✓ in progress
2. Create config pinned beads on rig init
3. Add `bd config get/set` commands that read/write pinned beads
4. Migrate existing config.json fields

## Naming Convention

```
<prefix>-cfg-<scope>
gt-cfg-theme      # gastown theme
bd-cfg-merge      # beads merge settings
gm-cfg-town       # town-level config
```

## Dependencies

- beads-6v2: Add StatusPinned to beads schema
- gm-w13: Pinned Beads epic","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-18T21:58:59.898116-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T21:58:59.898116-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-wpg","title":"Replaceable notifications via Claude Code queue","description":"Leverage Claude Code's ability to replace queued text for notifications that supersede previous ones.

## Problem

If daemon sends 10 heartbeats while agent is busy, agent returns to see 10 stacked messages. Wasteful and noisy.

## Solution

Use Claude Code's queue replacement for:
- Heartbeat messages (only latest matters)
- Status updates that supersede previous
- Progress notifications

## Implementation

Notifications get a 'slot' identifier. New notification in same slot replaces old one:
- Slot: 'heartbeat' → only one heartbeat queued at a time
- Slot: 'status-<rig>' → latest status per rig
- No slot → stacks normally (for unique messages)

## Research Needed

- How does Claude Code expose queue replacement?
- tmux send-keys behavior with pending input
- Alternative: clear + resend pattern","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:29.821949-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T13:19:00.398942-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T13:19:00.398942-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-wpg","depends_on_id":"gt-99m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:46.656972-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-wrw2","title":"Test2","description":"Testing gt mail","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"message","assignee":"gastown-alpha","created_at":"2025-12-20T21:39:05.875792-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T21:39:05.875792-08:00","labels":["thread:thread-1fd9f932cef0"],"sender":"Steve Yegge","wisp":true}
 {"id":"gt-wusk","title":"Layered context onboarding pattern","description":"Pattern from handoff discussion:

## Pattern: Layered Context Onboarding

Town CLAUDE.md (user/org) -> Rig CLAUDE.md (project) -> Role priming

## Ultra-compressed HOP for workers (no reveal)

- Permanent record: All work tracked. Outcomes matter.
- Quality gates: Molecule steps exist for a reason.
- Attribution: Completions build your track record.
- Handoff clean: Leave state any worker can continue.

## Recommendation

Create Town @AGENTS.md for shared worker context that all workers see.

 {"id":"gt-ynp6","title":"gt context --usage: estimate remaining context via tmux line count","description":"## Summary

Implement `gt context --usage` to estimate remaining context capacity.

## Approach

Use tmux scrollback buffer line count as proxy for token usage:
- `tmux capture-pane -p | wc -l` gives current line count
- Heuristics (tunable per model):
  - Opus 4.5: ~1800 lines = warning threshold
  - ~2000+ lines = critical, need handoff

## Output Format
```
Context Usage: 1642 lines (~82%)
Status: WARNING - consider handoff soon
```

## Notes
- Heuristics are model-specific, need config
- Line count is proxy, not exact token count
- Good enough for autonomous patrol decisions","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-23T01:19:13.108332-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T01:19:13.108332-08:00"}
+{"id":"gt-yt6g","title":"Standardize session end: gt handoff for all roles","description":"## Summary

Standardize session ending across all agent roles to use `gt handoff` as the canonical command. This is critical for the beads propulsion cycle - turning agent sessions from pets into cattle.

## Current State (Inconsistent)

| Role | Current Guidance | Command |
|------|-----------------|---------|
| Mayor | Manual mail send | `gt mail send mayor/ -s 'HANDOFF:...' -m '...'` |
| Crew | Manual mail send | `gt mail send <rig>/crew/<name> -s 'HANDOFF:...' -m '...'` |
| Witness | Manual mail send | `gt mail send <rig>/witness -s 'HANDOFF:...' -m '...'` |
| Refinery | Manual mail send | `gt mail send <rig>/refinery -s 'HANDOFF:...' -m '...'` |
| Deacon | Exit on high context | (implicit) |
| Polecat | `gt done` | `gt done [--exit TYPE]` |

## Target State (Unified)

All roles use `gt handoff`:
- `gt handoff` - Hand off current session to fresh instance
- `gt handoff -s 'context' -m 'details'` - Hand off with custom message
- For polecats: `gt handoff` internally calls `gt done`

## Changes Required

### 1. Code Changes
- [ ] Update `gt handoff` to detect polecat role and call `gt done` internally
- [ ] Consider adding `--exit` flag to `gt handoff` for polecat compatibility

### 2. CLAUDE.md Updates (gastown)
- [ ] ~/gt/CLAUDE.md (Mayor)
- [ ] gastown/crew/max/CLAUDE.md
- [ ] gastown/crew/joe/CLAUDE.md
- [ ] gastown/witness/CLAUDE.md
- [ ] gastown/refinery/CLAUDE.md (and rig/)
- [ ] deacon/CLAUDE.md

### 3. CLAUDE.md Updates (beads)
- [ ] beads/mayor/rig/CLAUDE.md
- [ ] beads/crew/dave/CLAUDE.md
- [ ] beads/crew/zoey/CLAUDE.md
- [ ] beads/witness/CLAUDE.md
- [ ] beads/refinery/CLAUDE.md (and rig/)

### 4. Architecture Docs
- [ ] docs/patrol-system-design.md
- [ ] gastown/mayor/rig/docs/prompts.md
- [ ] gastown/mayor/rig/docs/session-management.md

## New Session End Checklist (Universal)

```
# SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL

[ ] 1. git status              (check uncommitted changes)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage changes)
[ ] 3. git commit -m '...'     (commit with issue ID)
[ ] 4. bd sync                 (sync beads)
[ ] 5. git push                (push to remote - CRITICAL)
[ ] 6. gt handoff              (hand off to fresh session)
       OR gt handoff -s 'Context' -m 'Details for next session'
```

## Why This Matters

The handoff mechanism is what turns agent sessions from **pets** (precious, long-lived) into **cattle** (disposable, replaceable). At any time, any agent can:
1. Send itself a detailed handoff mail (or sling itself a mol)
2. System shuts them down and restarts them
3. Fresh session runs priming and reads mail
4. Work continues seamlessly

This enables:
- Unlimited context through automatic cycling
- Clean recovery from any state
- Consistent behavior across all roles
- Simplified agent instructions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T12:57:25.246279-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:05:12.92201-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T13:05:12.92201-08:00","close_reason":"Standardized session end guidance across all roles to use gt handoff as canonical method"}
 {"id":"gt-yx4","title":"Town root .beads has schema mismatch with bd","description":"The .beads directory at town root (/Users/stevey/gt/.beads) has an incompatible schema:

```
Error: failed to open database: failed to initialize schema: sqlite3: SQL logic error: no such column: thread_id
```

Meanwhile, gastown/.beads (symlinked to mayor/rig/.beads) works fine.

## Impact

- gt mail inbox fails at town root
- gt handoff sends mail to broken db
- Daemon can't check its inbox

## Options

1. Delete town root .beads/beads.db and let it recreate
2. Symlink town root .beads to gastown/.beads
3. Run schema migration on existing db

## Root Cause

Likely a beads version upgrade that added thread_id column, but the town root db was created before that.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:31:35.559042-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T00:39:32.211083-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T00:39:32.211083-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-z3qf","title":"Overhaul gt mol to match bd mol chemistry interface","description":"## The Sling: Unified Work Dispatch

This issue tracks the overhaul of `gt molecule` to align with chemistry metaphor and introduce the **Universal Gas Town Propulsion Principle**.

### The Propulsion Principle

> **If you find something on your hook, YOU RUN IT.**

This is the one rule that drives all Gas Town agents.

### The Sling Operation

`gt sling <thing> <target> [options]` - unified command for spawn + assign + pin.

See: `gastown/mayor/rig/docs/sling-design.md`

### Implementation Tasks

| Issue | Title | Priority |
|-------|-------|----------|
| gt-4ev4 | Implement gt sling command | P1 |
| gt-uym5 | Implement gt mol status command | P1 |
| gt-i4kq | Update templates for Propulsion Principle | P1 |
| gt-7hor | Document the Propulsion Principle | P2 |

### Command Changes

| Old | New |
|-----|-----|
| `gt molecule instantiate` | `gt sling` |
| `gt molecule attach` | `gt sling --force` |
| `gt molecule detach` | `gt mol burn` |
| `gt molecule progress` | `gt mol status` |
| `gt molecule list` | `gt mol catalog` |
| `gt spawn --molecule` | `gt sling` |

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `gt sling` works for protos, issues, and epics
- [ ] `gt mol status` shows hook state
- [ ] Templates updated for propulsion principle
- [ ] Old commands deprecated with warnings
- [ ] Documentation complete","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T03:02:38.049324-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:37:37.562677-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:37:37.562677-08:00","close_reason":"Core sling work complete (gt-4ev4, gt-uym5, gt-7hor closed). gt-i4kq (template updates) remains open but is independent polish work.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-z3qf","depends_on_id":"gt-4ev4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T12:10:42.394653-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-z3qf","depends_on_id":"gt-uym5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T12:10:42.46834-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-z3qf","depends_on_id":"gt-i4kq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T12:10:42.541384-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-z3qf","depends_on_id":"gt-7hor","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T12:10:42.613099-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-zivp","title":"mol-outpost-assign: Intelligent work routing to outposts","description":"The federation design shows outposts.yaml with a static policy section. Simple preference ordering is fine as config, but intelligent work routing should be a molecule.

Current static approach:
```yaml
policy:
  default_preference: [local, gce-burst, cloudrun-burst]
```

Cases requiring cognition:
- "This is a long-running research task, route to VM not CloudRun"
- "This touches sensitive code, keep local"
- "These 5 issues are related, batch them to same outpost"
- "CloudRun cost is high today, prefer local even if slower"

## Molecule: outpost-assign
Intelligent work-to-outpost routing.

## Step: classify-work
Analyze the issue/work item:
- Expected duration (quick fix vs multi-hour)
- Resource requirements
- Sensitivity/security tier
- Related work (same epic?)

## Step: check-capacity
Query outpost status:
- Current load on each
- Cost accrued today
- Health status

## Step: select-outpost
Choose optimal outpost based on:
- Work classification
- Capacity/cost
- Policy constraints
Needs: classify-work, check-capacity

## Step: emit-assignment
Record decision in beads for audit.
Needs: select-outpost

## Notes
- This molecule is invoked by Mayor/Witness when spawning
- Simple cases can short-circuit to static policy
- Full analysis only for ambiguous cases","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:26:17.964834-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T03:26:17.964834-08:00"}
